question_text: Let X follow a 10-dimensional multivariate normal distribution with zero mean vector and identity covariance matrix. Define Y = loge XTX and let MY (t) denote the moment generating function of Y at t, t > −10. Then MY (2) equals (
solution:
Step 1: Distribution of XTX
Since X ∼N10(0, I), it follows that:
XTX =
i=1
i ∼χ2
Step 2: Relation to Y
Given:
Y = 1
2 log(XTX) ⇒e2Y = XTX
Step 3: Moment Generating Function
MY (2) = E[e2Y ] = E[XTX]
Since XTX ∼χ2
10, and the expected value of a chi-squared distribution with 10 degrees of
freedom is 10:
E[XTX] = 10
Final Answer:
Quick Tip
When dealing with the moment generating function of transformations of multivariate
normal distributions, use known formulas and properties of chi-squared distributions to
simplify your calculations.
